topic_title: Escalating costs and military risks threaten Middle East truce between US and Iran
topic_summary: Reports indicate the cost of the conflict has exceeded $29 billion as a fragile truce faces collapse. While Iran has issued conditions for renewed negotiations, the US administration suggests economic pain is secondary to stopping Iran's nuclear program, and the UAE has reportedly launched a secret attack that risks drawing more Gulf states into the war.

findings.title: Pentagon lowballs Iran war cost at $29 billion as real toll could hit $1 trillion
findings.summary: A senior Pentagon official has revealed that the war on Iran has already cost the United States approximately $29 billion since February 28.
findings.text: Pentagon lowballs Iran war cost at $29 billion as real toll could hit $1 trillion | A senior Pentagon official has revealed that the war on Iran has already cost the United States approximately $29 billion since February 28.

findings.title: Morning update
findings.summary:
Morning update

    
    

        
    
                Good morning Middle East Eye readers,

Diplomatic activity around the Iran conflict is intensifying as Donald Trump heads to Beijing for talks with Chinese President Xi Jinping, saying he wants a “good deal” with Tehran while ruling out an Iranian nuclear program. 

China is also pushing Pakistan to continue mediation efforts between Washington and Tehran, even as fighting and military pressure continue across the region.

Meanwhil...

findings.title: US offers $15m reward over information on Iran-linked oil transfers
findings.summary:
US offers $15m reward over information on Iran-linked oil transfers

    
    

        
    
                The United States Department of State announced a reward of up to $15 million for information related to oil shipments connected to the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) and its facilitators.

In a statement issued through the Rewards for Justice Program, Washington said it was seeking information about IRGC-linked oil deliveries, the tankers and entities involved, and individu...

findings.title: La ‘cuenta’ de la guerra en Irán: Bloqueo y bombardeos cuestan 29 mil mdd a EU
findings.summary: Pete Hegseth, secretario de Guerra de Donald Trump, no descartó que EU pueda 'subir la tensión' con Irán ante el impasse entre ambos países en las negociaciones de un acuerdo de paz.

findings.title: Cost of US war on Iran mounting – as it happened
findings.summary: Pete Hegseth and other officials appear before House to face grilling on Iran war expenditure and military operationsIran has expanded its definition of the strait of Hormuz into a “vast operational area” far wider than before the war, according to a senior officer in the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) Navy in comments likely to anger the US.The strait is no longer viewed as a narrow stretch around a handful of islands but instead has been greatly enlarged in scope and military sign...

findings.title: UAE’s secret attack on Iran risks drawing Gulf states into the war
findings.summary: If current precarious ceasefire between US and Iran ends, Emirates are more likely to be targeted by TehranThe risk of some Gulf states becoming embroiled in a direct war with Iran has risen after it was reported the United Arab Emirates had secretly launched a major attack on Iran during the conflict.In addition, Kuwait has said that at least four members of the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps had been captured trying to carry out “terrorist attacks” on the Kuwaiti-owned Bubiyan Island, th...

findings.title: Middle East live: Trump says stopping Iran's nuclear programme outweighs Americans' economic pain
findings.summary: US President Donald ⁠Trump said he expects to have a "long talk" about Iran with his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping when he arrives in Beijing on Wednesday for a highly anticipated summit. Trump said Americans’ financial struggles were not a factor in his decision-making as he seeks to negotiate an end ​to the war, adding that preventing Tehran from acquiring a nuclear weapon is his top priority. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.
findings.text: Middle East live: Trump says stopping Iran's nuclear programme outweighs Americans' economic pain | US President Donald ⁠Trump said he expects to have a "long talk" about Iran with his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping when he arrives in Beijing on Wednesday for a highly anticipated summit. Trump said Americans’ financial struggles were not a factor in his decision-making as he seeks to negotiate an end ​to the war, adding that preventing Tehran from acquiring a nuclear weapon is his top priority. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.

findings.title: Iran warns US to accept peace plan or risk collapse of Middle East truce
findings.summary: Iran’s chief negotiator said on Tuesday that Washington must accept Tehran’s latest peace plan or risk the collapse of the Middle East truce, after President Donald Trump warned the ceasefire was near failure. The war, triggered by US-Israeli strikes on Iran more than two months ago, has destabilised the region and rattled global markets.
findings.text: Iran warns US to accept peace plan or risk collapse of Middle East truce | Iran’s chief negotiator said on Tuesday that Washington must accept Tehran’s latest peace plan or risk the collapse of the Middle East truce, after President Donald Trump warned the ceasefire was near failure. The war, triggered by US-Israeli strikes on Iran more than two months ago, has destabilised the region and rattled global markets.

findings.summary: The Tehran regime is more hard-line than ever—and has nothing left to lose.
